question_title: Which will be higher in the first official ISM reports for April 2026: the Manufacturing PMI or the Services PMI?
summary: The ISM Services PMI has been higher than Manufacturing PMI in all 27 months from January 2024 through March 2026 (100% base rate). The current gap is 1.3 points (Services 54.0 vs Manufacturing 52.7 in March 2026). Even when the gap narrowed below 2.0 (9 instances), the following month never saw a reversal — the gap typically widened back. While month-over-month volatility models suggest ~33% chance of reversal from a 1.3-point gap, the structural persistence and mean-reversion pattern substantially reduce this probability. Manufacturing faces slowing demand momentum (new orders decelerating from 55.8 to 53.5) and continued employment contraction, while Services maintains strong demand fundamentals (new orders at 60.6). Both sectors face headwinds from Middle East conflict and tariff uncertainty. I set Services PMI higher at 87%, Manufacturing higher at 11%, and Equal at 2%.

factors.description: Services PMI has been higher than Manufacturing PMI in ALL 27 months from Jan 2024 to Mar 2026 — a 100% base rate. The structural gap reflects the US economy's service-dominated nature.
Even when the gap narrowed below 2.0 (9 instances), the next month NEVER saw Manufacturing exceed Services — the gap typically widened back. Strong mean-reversion toward the positive structural gap.
Current gap is 1.3 (March 2026: Services 54.0 vs Manufacturing 52.7) — among the narrower gaps but still meaningfully positive.

Manufacturing demand momentum is decelerating: new orders slowed from 55.8 to 53.5 (still expanding but weakening), export orders contracting at 49.9, employment still in contraction at 48.7. ISM Chair expressed concern about demand direction.
Services had strong new orders (60.6) despite headline PMI drop. Demand fundamentals remain solid in services.

MoM volatility is high enough (std of gap change ~2.75) that a reversal from 1.3 is arithmetically possible (~33% from pure volatility modeling), but structural persistence heavily reduces this probability.
Geopolitical shocks (Middle East conflict, tariff policy) could differentially impact sectors, but manufacturing typically bears more direct tariff burden and supply chain disruption.
Exact equality at one decimal place is extremely unlikely given continuous underlying distributions and typical spread between the two indices.

reasoning:
**Final Forecast:**

| Outcome | Probability |
|---------|-------------|
| Services PMI higher | **87%** |
| Manufacturing PMI higher | **11%** |
| Equal | **2%** |

**Key reasoning:**
- **100% base rate**: Services PMI has exceeded Manufacturing PMI in every month since at least January 2024 (27 consecutive months)
- **Mean-reversion after narrow gaps**: Even when the gap narrowed to as little as 0.2 points, it never reversed — it widened back in all 9 observable cases
- **Current gap of 1.3 points** provides a meaningful buffer, though it's among the narrower readings
- **Demand divergence**: Manufacturing new orders are decelerating while Services new orders remain robust at 60.6
- **Volatility caveat**: Pure MoM change analysis gives ~33% reversal probability, but structural persistence strongly argues against that estimate
- The 11% for Manufacturing higher reflects genuine uncertainty while respecting the overwhelming historical pattern
sources_consulted: [US ISM Services PMI (Monthly) - United States - Historical …](https://ycharts.com/indicators/us_ism_non_manufacturing_index)
[(Metaculus) Which will be higher in the first official ISM reports for April 2026: the Manufacturing PMI or the Services PMI?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43137)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves to the option Manufacturing PMI higher if the first official April 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI is greater than the first official April 2026 ISM Services PMI. This question resolves to the option Services PMI higher if the first official April 2026 ISM Services PMI is greater than the first official April 2026 ISM Manufacturing PMI. This question resolves to the option Equal if the two headline values are identical. The primary source is ISM's PMI reports hub at https://www.ismworld.org/supply-management-news-and-reports/reports/ism-pmi-reports/
fine_print: Use the headline PMI values for April 2026 only, not subindexes. If ISM displays the values to one decimal place, compare the published one-decimal values. Candidate Sources: ISM release calendar https://www.ismworld.org/supply-management-news-and-reports/reports/rob-report-calendar/ ; ISM Manufacturing PMI April release page when posted under the same report hub
